edges.claim: ADQ committed approximately USD 35 billion for the Ras el-Hekma development, the largest FDI in Egyptian history, with Egypt holding a 35% stake with profit participation.
UAE–Egypt CEPA negotiations were being finalised as announced on 29 December 2025, within a UAE CEPA programme whose agreements contributed Dh135bn to Dh3 trillion non-oil foreign trade in 2024.
ADFD is financing Sheikh Zayed City infrastructure development in Egypt.
DP World signed free-zone MoUs in Egypt's New Administrative Capital in June 2026.
The Egypt–UAE Bilateral Investment Treaty (1997) is in force, providing the legal framework for bilateral investment flows.

The bilateral relationship operates through formal frameworks: an in-force 1997 investment treaty and a CEPA in final negotiation announced December 2025, together forming the UAE–Egypt strategic economic architecture.

GAP: No direct evidence of UAE development aid or ODA flows to Egypt specifically; Egypt is only named in European Commission coverage of Gulf-linked humanitarian and development activity without UAE-specific amounts, programmes, or dates.
GAP: No evidence in the material on the UAE–Egypt remittance corridor — no remittance volumes, worker population figures, or corridor data are documented.

GAP: No evidence of joint UAE–Egypt participation in a named multilateral program; the material documents UAE multilateral engagement (Gaza maritime corridor, Sudan co-chair mechanism, ADB) but does not place Egypt in any of these mechanisms.

MIDAR and Majid Al Futtaim signed a USD 3.1bn+ New Cairo mixed-use development deal on 21 June 2026 (total development value over USD 4bn, ~6,000 residential units); MAF cumulative Egypt investment is ~USD 2.8bn over 27 years.

The EU allocated €8 million of its €458 million 2026 Middle East humanitarian package to Egypt, and Egypt is named in European Commission coverage of Gulf-linked humanitarian and development activity.
AD Ports Group and Masdar formed a partnership in November 2025 for global offshore wind logistics.

The UAE–India CEPA (signed Feb 2022) drove bilateral trade to USD 100 billion in FY2024-25 with a USD 200bn target by 2032, serving as the benchmark template for CEPA impact including the Egypt negotiation.
The Tony Elumelu Foundation partnered with the UAE Office of Development Affairs and the Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation to empower an additional 1,000 young African entrepreneurs.

The UAE and the European Commission maintain a working humanitarian-logistics dialogue, including a DG ECHO co-hosted Brussels panel on supply-chain resilience (11 June 2026, 80+ senior representatives) and joint co-signature of the Gaza maritime corridor ministerial statement.

The UAE has provided more than $3 billion in aid to Gaza since 2023, delivering over 90,000 tons via 8,700 trucks, 80 airdrops and 13 ships, with UAE-supported facilities treating 75,000 Gazans, under active operations including Chivalrous Knight 3 and Birds of Goodness.

The UAE announced a $30 million Sudan emergency response and pledged $500 million to a Sudan Humanitarian Fund in February 2026, and co-chairs the Sudan de-escalation ministerial mechanism with the EU, Qatar and Kenya.
The UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned the targeting of a WFP convoy in Sudan and publicly supports WFP humanitarian operations in Sudan.

ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD, leads the UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative targeting digital infrastructure across Africa, linking UAE development finance to African digital infrastructure in a G20 context.

The UAE and Qatar both co-signed the Gaza maritime corridor ministerial joint statement and jointly co-chair the Sudan de-escalation mechanism, while operating parallel institutionalized aid agencies.

Theyab bin Mohamed bin Zayed — chairman of the restructured International Humanitarian and Philanthropic Council under Federal Decree No. 94 of 2026 — attended the International Development Dialogue hosted by the Office of Development Affairs.
Egypt is named in European Commission coverage of Gulf-linked humanitarian and development activity, situating Egypt within reporting on UAE-linked regional aid.
UAE-operated medical infrastructure in and around Gaza — field hospitals (54,000 patients treated) and a floating hospital (24,000 patients treated) — has provided care to 75,000 Gazans overall.
The UAE and the EU co-chaired the 24 September 2025 ministerial meeting coordinating Sudan de-escalation efforts as part of a joint co-chair mechanism.
A 2024 academic study in The International Spectator comparatively analyses Qatar and the UAE as rival Gulf donors in Sudan.
The UAE and Kenya co-chaired the 24 September 2025 ministerial meeting on Sudan de-escalation within a joint co-chair mechanism alongside the EU and Qatar.
The UAE sent 30 tonnes of direct bilateral food aid to Kenya in February 2026 in response to La Niña-driven drought and deepening food insecurity.
Dubai Humanitarian participated in the June 2026 UAE–EU supply-chain resilience discussions in Brussels co-hosted by DG ECHO.
The UAE and the United Kingdom co-signed the joint ministerial statement advancing a maritime corridor for humanitarian assistance to Gaza.
The UAE and the United States co-signed the joint ministerial statement advancing a Gaza humanitarian maritime corridor.
The UAE and Cyprus co-signed the joint ministerial statement advancing a Gaza humanitarian maritime corridor.

ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD, leads the UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative targeting digital infrastructure across Africa, announced in a G20 context and linking UAE development finance to African digital infrastructure.
The UAE foreign-aid agency co-leads the $1 billion AI for Development initiative with ADEX, ADFD's export-financing arm.
The UAE contributed $1.5 million in May 2026, via the International Affairs Office at the UAE Presidential Court, to an ADB fund promoting agricultural innovation and Asia-Pacific food security.
The Office of Development Affairs and the Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation jointly co-fund African entrepreneurship empowerment with the Tony Elumelu Foundation.
The Office of Development Affairs at the UAE Presidential Court held an event with the UAE Embassy in Beijing in July 2026.
UN OCHA's Financial Tracking Service independently tracks the UAE (donor #100), recording $94,336,382 in funded and $133,015,246 in paid UAE humanitarian flows for 2026.

The UAE President established the UAE Aid Agency via Federal Decree No. 27 of 2024, as announced by the UAE National Media Authority; the agency operates an open-data transparency portal at uaeaid.ae and published the UAE Foreign Aid Report 2024.
